spots.title: Wire the deployment manifest to the DeepBook SDK client
spots.prompt:
The deployment manifest has been fetched. Now you need to wire it into the DeepBook SDK client.

Your task: implement the three helper functions (`packageIds`, `coinMap`, `poolMap`) that extract the SDK configuration from the manifest, then build the `SuiGrpcClient` and attach the `deepbook` extension.

You are configuring for pool: {{ pool_subset }}.

Lines 39–58 of `src/App.tsx` are the target. Use `packageIds(manifest)`, `coinMap(manifest)`, and `poolMap(manifest)` as arguments to the `deepbook(...)` call.

spots.title: Add the withRetry helper for resilient gRPC calls
spots.prompt:
The DeepBook gRPC SimulateTransaction endpoint occasionally returns without commandResults at block boundaries or when the node is busy. You need to implement a `withRetry` helper that retries a failing async operation up to N times with a brief pause between attempts.

Your task: implement `withRetry<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>, retries = 2): Promise<T>` at lines 103–114 of `src/App.tsx`. The helper should iterate `retries` times, return on the first success, add a 300ms pause before each retry (except the first attempt), and re-throw on the final failure.

phases.title: Phase 3 — Polling Loop with Failure Tolerance

spots.title: Implement the polling tick using withRetry
spots.prompt:
Now implement the `tick` function and the polling loop that fetches mid price and level 2 ticks from the DeepBook SDK. The loop must use `withRetry` for each gRPC call and only surface errors to the UI after 3 consecutive failures.

Your task: implement `tick()` at lines 116–145 of `src/App.tsx`. Call `withRetry(() => client!.deepbook.midPrice(pool))` and `withRetry(() => client!.deepbook.getLevel2TicksFromMid(pool, 10))` via `Promise.all`, update the `book` and `err` state, and start the polling interval with `setInterval(tick, {{ poll_interval_ms }})`. Cancel via `clearInterval` in the cleanup function.
